- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Data-sovereignty helper: enforce that network endpoints are loopback-only (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every engine component that takes a host/URL for an on-box service (local model runtime,
4	tracing collector, vector store, checkpoint database) routes it through here so a non-local
5	address fails loud rather than silently shipping data off the machine.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import ipaddress
11	import re
12	from urllib.parse import parse_qs, urlparse
13	
14	# A libpq keyword/value token: ``key = value`` with optional whitespace around ``=`` (libpq
15	# permits it) and an optionally single/double-quoted value (which may contain spaces).
16	_DSN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"(\w+)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
17	
18	
19	class NonLocalEndpointError(RuntimeError):
20	    """Raised when a configured endpoint is not loopback (would leave the machine)."""
21	
22	
23	def is_loopback_host(host: str | None) -> bool:
24	    """True only for genuine loopback hosts.
25	
26	    Parses the host as an IP and tests the loopback network (127.0.0.0/8, ::1), so a public
27	    name that merely *starts with* ``127.`` (e.g. ``127.0.0.1.evil.com``) is correctly
28	    rejected. The literal ``localhost`` is the only accepted non-IP host.
29	    """
30	    if not host:
31	        return False
32	    h = host.strip().lower()
33	    if h == "localhost":
34	        return True
35	    try:
36	        return ipaddress.ip_address(h.strip("[]")).is_loopback
37	    except ValueError:
38	        return False
39	
40	
41	def require_loopback_url(url: str, *, what: str) -> str:
42	    """Return ``url`` if its host is loopback; else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`."""
43	    host = urlparse(url).hostname
44	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
45	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
46	            f"{what} must be loopback-only (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got host {host!r} "
47	            f"from {url!r}"
48	        )
49	    return url
50	
51	
52	def require_loopback_host(host: str, *, what: str) -> str:
53	    """Return ``host`` if it is loopback; else raise. (``0.0.0.0`` binds all interfaces and
54	    is correctly rejected — it would expose data off-box.)"""
55	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
56	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
57	            f"{what} must be a loopback host (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got {host!r}"
58	        )
59	    return host
60	
61	
62	def _authority_hosts(netloc: str) -> list[str]:
63	    """Every host in a URI authority, supporting libpq's comma-separated multi-host form.
64	
65	    ``urlparse().hostname`` returns only the *first* host of a multi-host authority
66	    (``host1:port1,host2:port2``), so a remote second host would slip past a single-host
67	    check (audit #D3). Each host may carry its own ``:port`` and IPv6 hosts are bracketed.
68	
69	    Userinfo is stripped at the **first** ``@`` — matching libpq, which (unlike ``urlparse``,
70	    which uses the last ``@``) treats the first ``@`` as the userinfo delimiter. A string like
71	    ``[REDACTED]:5432@localhost`` is therefore read as host ``evil.com`` here, not
72	    ``localhost`` (audit #D5). Any host element still containing ``@`` after that is ambiguous
73	    and is returned verbatim so the caller's loopback check fails it closed.
74	    """
75	    if "@" in netloc:
76	        netloc = netloc.split("@", 1)[1]
77	    hosts: list[str] = []
78	    for part in netloc.split(","):
79	        p = part.strip()
80	        if not p:
81	            continue
82	        if p.startswith("["):  # [ipv6] or [ipv6]:port
83	            end = p.find("]")
84	            hosts.append(p[1:end] if end != -1 else p[1:])
85	        else:
86	            hosts.append(p.rsplit(":", 1)[0] if ":" in p else p)
87	    return hosts
88	
89	
90	def require_local_uri(uri: str, *, what: str) -> str:
91	    """Return ``uri`` if it is local (ADR-0003), else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`.
92	
93	    Three forms are recognized, validated against what the real client (libpq for postgres,
94	    urllib for http, sqlite for files) actually connects to — not merely what ``urlparse``
95	    reports, since the two can disagree:
96	
97	    - **URI with an authority** (``scheme://host…`` or scheme-relative ``//host…``): *every*
98	      host in the authority must be loopback (incl. each of a comma-separated multi-host
99	      authority, #D3), AND any ``host``/``hostaddr`` query parameter that libpq would use to
100	      override the authority host (#D2). ``hostaddr`` is checked independently of ``host``.
101	    - **libpq keyword/value DSN** (no authority but ``key=value`` tokens): ``host``/``hostaddr``
102	      must be loopback, tolerating libpq's whitespace/quoting around ``=`` (#P1, #D1).
103	    - **bare path** (no authority, no ``=``): a local file/dir — but a Windows UNC path
104	      (``\\\\server\\share``) is rejected as remote.
105	
106	    Inputs that cannot be parsed safely fail closed (rejected), never accepted.
107	    """
108	    try:
109	        parsed = urlparse(uri)
110	        netloc = parsed.netloc
111	        query = parsed.query
112	    except ValueError:
113	        # e.g. a malformed IPv6 authority — we cannot determine the host, so refuse.
114	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
115	            f"{what}: endpoint could not be parsed safely; rejecting (ADR-0003)"
116	        ) from None
117	
118	    if netloc:
119	        # A backslash never legitimately appears in a URI authority; a WHATWG-normalizing
120	        # client (e.g. some HTTP stacks) treats '\' as '/', so it could read a different host
121	        # than urlparse. Refuse rather than risk that differential (audit #D6, defense-in-depth).
122	        if "\\" in netloc:
123	            raise NonLocalEndpointError(
124	                f"{what}: backslash in URI authority is not permitted (ADR-0003); rejecting"
125	            )
126	        for h in _authority_hosts(netloc):
127	            # A residual '@' means the authority is ambiguous between this parser and libpq —
128	            # fail closed (audit #D5).
129	            if "@" in h or not is_loopback_host(h):
130	                raise NonLocalEndpointError(
131	                    f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); authority "
132	                    f"names non-loopback host {h!r}"
133	                )
134	        for key in ("host", "hostaddr"):
135	            for val in parse_qs(query).get(key, []):
136	                h = val.strip()
137	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h):
138	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
139	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); URI query "
140	                        f"{key}={h!r} points off-box"
141	                    )
142	        return uri
143	
144	    if "=" in uri:  # libpq keyword/value DSN (no URI authority)
145	        for match in _DSN_TOKEN_RE.finditer(uri):
146	            if match.group(1).strip().lower() in {"host", "hostaddr"}:
147	                h = match.group(2).strip().strip("'\"")
148	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h):
149	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
150	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); DSN names "
151	                        f"non-loopback host {h!r}"
152	                    )
153	        return uri
154	
155	    stripped = uri.lstrip()
156	    # Windows treats ANY two leading separators (in any mix of '\' and '/') as a UNC prefix
157	    # to a remote SMB share — \\srv, //srv, /\srv, \/srv all resolve off-box (audit #D6).
158	    # (The pure '//host' form is already caught by the authority branch above; covering it
159	    # here too keeps the branch self-contained.)
160	    if len(stripped) >= 2 and stripped[0] in "\\/" and stripped[1] in "\\/":
161	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
162	            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); UNC-style path "
163	            f"{uri!r} is a remote share"
164	        )
165	    return uri  # bare path / dir store
166

1	"""Tiered reasoning seam — the single abstraction over LLM execution by stakes tier.
2	
3	No engine component talks to an LLM directly; everything routes through :class:`ReasoningSeam`.
4	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's hardened ``reasoning_client`` (ARCHITECTURE.md
5	§1, ADR-0003). Two execution tiers:
6	
7	- ``StakesTier.BULK`` — a local model runtime (loopback-only, e.g. Ollama) for high-volume,
8	  low-stakes work.
9	- ``StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES`` — a frontier model via a **fixed-cost subscription** path
10	  (the ``claude`` CLI / Agent SDK using an OAuth token). No metered API key is ever used:
11	  every metered Anthropic credential is scrubbed from the subprocess environment first.
12	
13	Hardened invariants preserved from the prior platform:
14	
15	- forward-proof credential scrubbing (any ``ANTHROPIC_*KEY``/``*TOKEN`` stripped; the
16	  subscription ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` preserved);
17	- hard per-call timeouts via a bounded worker pool with a saturation guard;
18	- independent retry budgets — exponential backoff for transient errors, long backpressure
19	  for rate limits — and permanent errors never retried;
20	- robust JSON extraction via a balanced-brace walker (not a greedy regex);
21	- loopback enforcement on the local backend (ADR-0003).
22	
23	Heavy backends (``langchain_ollama``, ``claude_agent_sdk``) and tracing (``opentelemetry``)
24	are imported lazily, so this module imports — and is unit-testable by mocking ``_attempt`` —
25	with none of them installed. Install the optional ``reasoning`` extra to run real calls.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations

105	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
106	# Credential scrubbing (ADR-0003 / no metered API) — pure, forward-proof
107	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
108	def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
109	    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK would read as a METERED credential.
110	
111	    Matches the *shape* — any ``ANTHROPIC_*`` var naming a ``KEY`` or ``TOKEN`` — rather than
112	    a static list, so a newly-added metered var cannot silently slip through. The subscription
113	    ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` does not start with ``ANTHROPIC_`` and is deliberately kept.
114	    """
115	    return key.startswith("ANTHROPIC_") and ("KEY" in key or "TOKEN" in key)

118	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
119	    """The process env with every metered Anthropic credential removed.
120	
121	    Execution must run on the fixed subscription via the CLI's OAuth token; a metered key, if
122	    present, would silently bill per token — so strip it before the SDK subprocess sees it.
123	    """
124	    return {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if not is_metered_anthropic_credential(k)}

127	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
128	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
129	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
130	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
131	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
132	_inflight = 0
133	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
134	
135	
136	def _release(_future: Any) -> None:
137	    global _inflight
138	    with _inflight_lock:
139	        _inflight -= 1
140	
141	
142	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
143	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
144	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
145	    global _inflight
146	    with _inflight_lock:
147	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
148	            raise PermanentReasoningError(
149	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
150	                "hung backend call(s) have leaked every worker slot"
151	            )
152	        _inflight += 1
153	    try:
154	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
155	    except BaseException:
156	        with _inflight_lock:
157	            _inflight -= 1
158	        raise
159	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
160	    return future
161	
162	
163	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
164	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
165	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
166	    try:
167	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
168	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
169	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
170	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
171	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
172	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
173

175	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
176	# JSON extraction — balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
177	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
178	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
179	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
180	
181	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
182	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
183	    """
184	    start = None
185	    opener = closer = ""
186	    depth = 0
187	    in_string = False
188	    escape = False
189	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
190	        if start is None:
191	            if ch in "{[":
192	                start, opener = i, ch
193	                closer = "}" if ch == "{" else "]"
194	                depth = 1
195	            continue
196	        if in_string:
197	            if escape:
198	                escape = False
199	            elif ch == "\\":
200	                escape = True
201	            elif ch == '"':
202	                in_string = False
203	            continue
204	        if ch == '"':
205	            in_string = True
206	        elif ch == opener:
207	            depth += 1
208	        elif ch == closer:
209	            depth -= 1
210	            if depth == 0:
211	                return text[start : i + 1]
212	    return None
213	
214	
215	def extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
216	    """Parse JSON from a model reply (handles code fences and surrounding prose)."""
217	    span = _first_balanced_json(text)
218	    if span is None:
219	        raise ReasoningError("no JSON object/array found in reply")
220	    try:
221	        return json.loads(span)
222	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
223	        raise ReasoningError(f"reply contained malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc
224

226	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
227	# Tier backends (lazy imports — module loads without them)
228	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
229	def _bulk_complete(
230	    prompt: str, system: str | None, temperature: float, model: str, json_mode: bool = False
231	) -> str:
232	    """Tier: BULK — local model runtime (loopback-enforced)."""
233	    require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
234	    try:
235	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
236	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
237	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
238	            "bulk tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (langchain-ollama) — not installed"
239	        ) from exc
240	
241	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = dict(
242	        model=model, base_url=BULK_BASE_URL, temperature=temperature,
243	        client_kwargs={"timeout": BULK_TIMEOUT},
244	    )
245	    if json_mode:
246	        kwargs["format"] = "json"  # Ollama native constrained JSON decoding
247	    client = ChatOllama(**kwargs)
248	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
249	    if system:
250	        messages.append(("system", system))
251	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
252	    try:
253	        reply = client.invoke(messages)
254	    except Exception as exc:
255	        msg = str(exc).lower()
256	        if "not found" in msg or "no such model" in msg or "try pulling" in msg:
257	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"bulk model {model!r} not available: {exc}") from exc
258	        raise ReasoningError(f"bulk tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
259	    content = getattr(reply, "content", reply)
260	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
261	    text = text.strip()
262	    if not text:  # an empty reply is not a valid result — retry, don't return "" (issue #5/R5)
263	        raise ReasoningError("bulk tier returned an empty reply")
264	    return text

267	def _high_stakes_auth_present() -> bool:
268	    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
269	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
270	    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
271	        return True
272	    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()
273	
274	
275	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
276	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
277	    try:
278	        import anyio
279	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
280	            AssistantMessage,
281	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
282	            ResultMessage,
283	            TextBlock,
284	            query,
285	        )
286	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
287	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
288	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
289	        ) from exc
290	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
291	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
292	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
293	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
294	        )
295	
296	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
297	        system_prompt=system,
298	        model=model,
299	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
300	        setting_sources=[],
301	        allowed_tools=[],
302	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
303	    )
304	
305	    parts: list[str] = []
306	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
307	
308	    async def _drain() -> None:
309	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
310	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
311	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
312	        nonlocal result_error
313	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
314	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
315	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
316	                if rl == "rejected":
317	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
318	                continue
319	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
320	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
321	                continue
322	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
323	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
324	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
325	                        parts.append(block.text)
326	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
327	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
328	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
329	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
330	                ):
331	                    result_error = {
332	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
333	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None)
334	                        or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
335	                    }
336	
337	    try:
338	        anyio.run(_drain)
339	    except Exception as exc:
340	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
341	
342	    if result_error is not None:
343	        status = result_error.get("status")
344	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
345	        if status in (429, 529):
346	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")
347	        if status in (401, 403):
348	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier auth failed ({status}): {detail}")
349	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
350	
351	    text = "".join(parts).strip()
352	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)
353	        raise ReasoningError("high-stakes tier returned an empty reply")
354	    return text
355	
356	
357	def _attempt(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
358	    """Route one request to its tier backend (no retry here)."""
359	    if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
360	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning is disabled (ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING)")
361	    p = request.params
362	    system = p.get("system")
363	    temperature = float(p.get("temperature", 0.0))
364	    model = p.get("model")
365	    json_mode = bool(p.get("_json_mode", False))
366	    if request.tier is StakesTier.BULK:
367	        return cast(
368	            str,
369	            _with_timeout(
370	                lambda: _bulk_complete(
371	                    request.prompt, system, temperature, model or BULK_MODEL, json_mode
372	                ),
373	                BULK_TIMEOUT + 10,
374	                "bulk tier",
375	            ),
376	        )
377	    if request.tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
378	        return cast(
379	            str,
380	            _with_timeout(
381	                lambda: _high_stakes_complete(request.prompt, system, model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL),
382	                HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30,
383	                "high-stakes tier",
384	            ),
385	        )
386	    raise PermanentReasoningError(f"unknown tier: {request.tier!r}")
387	
388	
389	def _run_with_retries(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
390	    """Execute with independent transient and rate-limit retry budgets."""
391	    transient = 0
392	    rate = 0
393	    while True:
394	        try:
395	            return _attempt(request)
396	        except PermanentReasoningError:
397	            raise
398	        except RateLimitError:
399	            if rate >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
400	                raise
401	            time.sleep(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate + 1))
402	            rate += 1
403	        except ReasoningError:
404	            if transient >= MAX_RETRIES:
405	                raise
406	            time.sleep(BACKOFF_BASE**transient)
407	            transient += 1
408

410	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
411	# Public seam
412	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
413	@runtime_checkable
414	class ReasoningSeam(Protocol):
415	    """Route a reasoning request to the appropriate tier with timeouts, retries, tracing."""
416	
417	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str: ...
418	
419	
420	@contextmanager
421	def _span(name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any]) -> Iterator[None]:
422	    """Emit an OpenTelemetry span if a provider is registered; a cheap no-op otherwise.
423	
424	    OpenTelemetry is imported lazily, so the seam traces when the (self-hosted, on-box)
425	    observability stack is wired and is a silent no-op in tests / offline (ADR-0003).
426	    """
427	    try:
428	        from opentelemetry import trace
429	    except ImportError:
430	        yield
431	        return
432	    tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine.reasoning")
433	    with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
434	        for k, v in attributes.items():
435	            span.set_attribute(k, v)
436	        yield
437	
438	
439	class TieredReasoningSeam:
440	    """Concrete tiered seam. ``run`` returns text; ``run_json`` parses a JSON reply.
441	
442	    Backends and tracing are resolved lazily, so an instance constructs with no external
443	    dependencies; calls fail loud (``PermanentReasoningError``) if a tier's backend or its
444	    subscription auth is absent. When the observability stack is present, each call emits an
445	    OpenTelemetry span; otherwise tracing is a no-op.
446	    """
447	
448	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
449	        attrs = {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose}
450	        with _span("reasoning.run", attrs):
451	            return _run_with_retries(request)
452	
453	    def run_json(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> Any:
454	        """Generate and parse JSON, regenerating with a varied temperature on parse failure.
455	
456	        BULK decoding can be deterministic (fixed seed, temp 0), so re-issuing the identical
457	        prompt would yield a byte-identical bad reply; each retry bumps the temperature.
458	        ``params`` is frozen, so each attempt builds a fresh request (issue #R6).
459	        """
460	        base_temp = float(request.params.get("temperature", 0.0))
461	        system = request.params.get("system")
462	        json_system = (
463	            f"{system}\n\nRespond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
464	            if system
465	            else "Respond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
466	        )
467	        last: ReasoningError | None = None
468	        with _span(
469	            "reasoning.run_json",
470	            {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose},
471	        ):
472	            for attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES + 1):
473	                bump = 0.2 * attempt
474	                temp = base_temp + bump if base_temp + bump <= 1.0 else max(0.0, base_temp - bump)
475	                params = dict(request.params)
476	                params.update(temperature=temp, system=json_system, _json_mode=True)
477	                attempt_req = ReasoningRequest(
478	                    prompt=request.prompt, tier=request.tier, purpose=request.purpose,
479	                    params=params,
480	                )
481	                try:
482	                    return extract_json(_run_with_retries(attempt_req))
483	                except ReasoningError as exc:
484	                    last = exc
485	            raise last if last is not None else ReasoningError("run_json failed")
486	
487	    @staticmethod
488	    def is_available(tier: StakesTier) -> bool:
489	        """Cheap liveness check (spends no LLM turn). Never raises — returns False on error."""
490	        if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
491	            return False
492	        try:
493	            if tier is StakesTier.BULK:
494	                require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
495	                import urllib.request
496	
497	                with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{BULK_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=2) as resp:
498	                    return bool(resp.status == 200)
499	            if tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
500	                import shutil
501	
502	                return shutil.which("claude") is not None and _high_stakes_auth_present()
503	        except Exception:
504	            return False
505	        return False
506

509	    """Explicit fail-loud seam for contexts that must not perform reasoning."""
510

512	        raise NotImplementedError(
513	            "this context uses an UnconfiguredReasoningSeam — supply a TieredReasoningSeam "
514	            "(or a test double) to perform reasoning"

1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
47	
48	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
49	
50	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
51	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
52	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
53	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
54	
55	# Guards the process-local one-time-init bookkeeping. The PG advisory lock serializes DDL
56	# across PROCESSES; this lock makes the in-process check-and-set sequences (atexit
57	# registration, the initialized-conn set) atomic so two threads can't double-register or
58	# double-setup.
59	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
60	
61	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
62	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
63	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
64	_atexit_registered = False
65	
66	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL) has already run in this
67	# process — bootstrap once per process per schema; the advisory lock still guards the
68	# first-time cross-process race.
69	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
70	
71	# Strips ``://user:password@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
72	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
73	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
74	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[^@\s]+@")
75	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=...`` / ``pgpassword=...``),
76	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
77	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
78	
79	
80	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
81	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
82	
83	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
84	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
85	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
86	    """
87	    try:
88	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
89	        host = u.hostname or ""
90	        port = u.port
91	        scheme = u.scheme
92	        path = u.path
93	    except ValueError:
94	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
95	    if not host:
96	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
97	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
98	        host = f"[{host}]"
99	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
100	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
101	
102	
103	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
104	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
105	
106	    Layers, in order of precision:
107	
108	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
109	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
110	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://user:pass@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
111	       *other* URI in the text;
112	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=``/``pgpassword=`` strip for the keyword/value form.
113	
114	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
115	    not pass as ``conn_str``; the primary connection string is always scrubbed precisely.
116	    """
117	    if conn_str:
118	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
119	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
120	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
121	
122	
123	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
124	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
125	
126	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
127	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
128	    """
129	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
130	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
131	
132	
133	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
134	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
135	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
136	    that can raise)."""
137	    try:
138	        pool.close()
139	    except Exception:
140	        pass
141	
142	
143	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
144	    for p in _OPEN_POOLS:
145	        _safe_close(p)
146	
147	
148	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
149	    global _atexit_registered
150	    with _init_lock:
151	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
152	        if not _atexit_registered:
153	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
154	            _atexit_registered = True
155	
156	
157	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
158	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
159	
160	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
161	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
162	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
163	    or the extra is not installed.
164	    """
165	    try:
166	        if use_memory:
167	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
168	
169	            return MemorySaver()
170	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
171	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
172	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
173	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
174	        raise RuntimeError(
175	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
176	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
177	        ) from exc
178	
179	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
180	    failure: str | None = None
181	    try:
182	        pool = ConnectionPool(
183	            conn_str,
184	            min_size=1,
185	            max_size=8,
186	            kwargs={"autocommit": True, "prepare_threshold": 0, "row_factory": dict_row},
187	            check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
188	            open=True,
189	        )
190	        _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
191	        # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
192	        # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
193	        # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
194	        with _init_lock:
195	            if conn_str not in _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS:
196	                with pool.connection() as conn:
197	                    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
198	                    try:
199	                        # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
200	                        # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
201	                        # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
202	                        PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
203	                    finally:
204	                        # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
205	                        # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
206	                        try:
207	                            conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
208	                        except Exception:
209	                            pass
210	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
211	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
212	    except Exception as exc:
213	        # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
214	        # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
215	        # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
216	        failure = (
217	            f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
218	            f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
219	            "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
220	        )
221	    raise RuntimeError(failure)
222	
223	
224	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
225	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
226	
227	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
228	    otherwise Postgres.
229	    """
230	    if use_memory is None:
231	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
232	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
233

1	"""Self-hosted tracing — OpenTelemetry → a local collector (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's Phoenix/OTel bootstrap. Everything is
4	on-box: spans export over OTLP to a loopback-bound collector; nothing leaves the machine.
5	OpenTelemetry and the Phoenix collector are optional (the ``observability`` extra) and
6	imported lazily, so this module loads — and the engine runs untraced — with neither present.
7	
8	The reasoning seam already emits spans through the global OTel tracer provider; calling
9	:func:`bootstrap_tracing` once at startup wires that global provider at a local collector so
10	those spans land somewhere. Without the extra (or with ``ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING`` set),
11	tracing degrades to a silent no-op.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import atexit
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import signal
20	import threading
21	from contextlib import contextmanager
22	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol, runtime_checkable
23	
24	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
25	
26	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.observability")
27	
28	# Local collector endpoint (loopback-enforced).
29	TRACING_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_HOST", "localhost")
30	TRACING_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006"))
31	PROJECT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PROJECT", "assay")
32	
33	_provider: Any = None
34	# Guards the _provider check-then-set so concurrent bootstrap_tracing() calls don't race.
35	_provider_lock = threading.Lock()
36	
37	
38	def _disabled() -> bool:
39	    return os.environ.get("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
40	
41	
42	def tracing_base_url() -> str:
43	    require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
44	    return f"http://{TRACING_HOST}:{TRACING_PORT}"
45	
46	
47	def tracing_endpoint() -> str:
48	    """OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint at the local collector (loopback-enforced)."""
49	    return f"{tracing_base_url()}/v1/traces"
50	
51	
52	def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
53	    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
54	
55	    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
56	    setup fails — tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
57	    """
58	    global _provider
59	    if _disabled():
60	        return None
61	    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
62	    with _provider_lock:
63	        if _provider is not None:
64	            return _provider
65	        try:
66	            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
67	            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
68	            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
69	            from phoenix.otel import register
70	
71	            provider = register(
72	                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
73	                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
74	                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
75	                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor — never block the run on export (#O2)
76	            )
77	            _provider = provider
78	            _warn_if_not_global(provider)
79	            _instrument_langchain()
80	            _install_flush_on_exit(provider)
81	            return _provider
82	        except Exception:
83	            # Missing extra / collector down / registration race — run untraced.
84	            return None
85	
86	
87	def _warn_if_not_global(provider: Any) -> None:
88	    """If another provider already won the global slot, our manual spans would vanish (#O6)."""
89	    try:
90	        from opentelemetry import trace
91	
92	        if trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
93	            _log.warning(
94	                "assay tracing: another OpenTelemetry provider holds the global slot; "
95	                "manually-emitted spans may not reach the local collector"
96	            )
97	    except Exception:
98	        pass
99	
100	
101	def _instrument_langchain() -> None:
102	    """Capture LangChain auto-spans (the bulk reasoning tier uses LangChain) — best-effort (#O5)."""
103	    try:
104	        from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
105	
106	        LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)
107	    except Exception:
108	        pass
109	
110	
111	def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
112	    """Flush buffered spans on clean exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM (#O3).
113	
114	    atexit does not run on ``kill``/container-stop, so a main-thread SIGTERM handler also
115	    flushes+shuts down. Both are guarded — tracing teardown must never raise.
116	    """
117	    flush = getattr(provider, "force_flush", None)
118	    if not callable(flush):
119	        return
120	    atexit.register(lambda: _safe(flush))
121	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
122	        try:
123	            prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
124	
125	            def _handler(signum: int, frame: Any) -> None:
126	                _safe(flush)
127	                _safe(getattr(provider, "shutdown", lambda: None))
128	                if callable(prior):
129	                    prior(signum, frame)
130	
131	            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
132	        except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - not on main thread / unsupported
133	            pass
134	
135	
136	def _safe(fn: Any) -> None:
137	    try:
138	        fn()
139	    except Exception:
140	        pass
141	
142	
143	@contextmanager
144	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
145	    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).
146	
147	    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
148	    """
149	    if run_id is None:
150	        yield
151	        return
152	    try:
153	        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
154	    except ImportError:
155	        yield
156	        return
157	    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
158	    try:
159	        yield
160	    finally:
161	        context.detach(token)
162	
163	
164	@runtime_checkable
165	class Tracer(Protocol):
166	    @contextmanager
167	    def span(self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> Iterator[None]:
168	        ...
169	
170	
171	class OtelTracer:
172	    """A tracer that emits OpenTelemetry spans via the global provider (no-op if OTel absent).
173	
174	    Use this where a component wants explicit spans rather than relying on auto-instrumentation;
175	    it honors whatever provider :func:`bootstrap_tracing` registered.
176	    """
177	
178	    @contextmanager
179	    def span(self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> Iterator[None]:
180	        try:
181	            from opentelemetry import trace
182	        except ImportError:
183	            yield
184	            return
185	        tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine")
186	        with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as sp:
187	            for k, v in (attributes or {}).items():
188	                sp.set_attribute(k, v)
189	            yield
190	
191	
192	class UnconfiguredTracer:
193	    """Fail-loud placeholder for contexts that must not silently drop spans."""
194	
195	    @contextmanager
196	    def span(self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> Iterator[None]:
197	        raise NotImplementedError(
198	            "no tracer configured — call bootstrap_tracing() and use OtelTracer, or supply a "
199	            "test double"
200	        )
201	        yield  # pragma: no cover - keeps this a generator/contextmanager
202

1	"""Analysis-graph assembly + run/resume — the goal-agnostic orchestration wiring.
2	
3	The concrete nodes and topology of a study's analysis graph are the study's (adapter's)
4	responsibility — they encode dataset specifics and so must not live in the engine (ADR-0002).
5	The engine provides the reusable, hardened mechanics around them:
6	
7	- compile a LangGraph ``StateGraph`` with the durable checkpointer attached (so a run can
8	  interrupt at a gate and resume later);
9	- bound non-converging gate-rejection loops with a recursion limit, so a run that never
10	  converges fails loud (``GraphRecursionError``) instead of looping unbounded;
11	- stamp ``run_id`` as the checkpointer ``thread_id`` and onto traces (cross-store
12	  correlation), inside a trace context;
13	- the gate interrupt/resume protocol with its correlation guard (see
14	  :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode`).
15	
16	LangGraph is an optional extra (``orchestration``, ADR-0006), imported lazily.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	from typing import Any, Callable
22	
23	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import bootstrap_tracing, run_trace_context
24	
25	# Upper bound on graph super-steps per run. Bounds a gate-rejection→re-analysis loop so a
26	# non-converging run terminates loudly instead of hanging.
27	RECURSION_LIMIT = 50
28	
29	
30	def _require_langgraph() -> Any:
31	    try:
32	        import langgraph  # noqa: F401
33	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
34	        raise RuntimeError(
35	            "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
36	        ) from exc
37	    return langgraph
38	
39	
40	def compile_graph(
41	    state_schema: Any,
42	    *,
43	    build: Callable[[Any], None],
44	    checkpointer: Any | None = None,
45	    requires_checkpointer: bool = False,
46	) -> Any:
47	    """Compile a study's analysis graph.
48	
49	    ``build(builder)`` is a study-supplied callback that adds the study's nodes, edges, and
50	    entry point to the LangGraph ``StateGraph`` builder; the engine owns construction and
51	    attaching the durable ``checkpointer``. Returns the compiled graph.
52	
53	    Set ``requires_checkpointer=True`` for any graph containing gate nodes: ``interrupt`` is a
54	    silent no-op without a checkpointer, so a gate graph compiled without one would never park
55	    for the operator. The engine fails loud rather than ship a silently-broken governance
56	    graph (audit #G3). The engine cannot introspect the study-supplied ``build`` callback for
57	    gate nodes, so this is the caller's responsibility: **any study wiring**
58	    :func:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode.make_gate_node` **must pass
59	    ``requires_checkpointer=True``** (see GOVERNANCE.md "Gate interrupt/resume protocol").
60	    """
61	    if requires_checkpointer and checkpointer is None:
62	        raise RuntimeError(
63	            "compile_graph(requires_checkpointer=True): a gate-bearing graph needs a durable "
64	            "checkpointer — interrupt() cannot park the run for the operator without one"
65	        )
66	    _require_langgraph()
67	    from langgraph.graph import StateGraph
68	
69	    builder = StateGraph(state_schema)
70	    build(builder)
71	    if checkpointer is not None:
72	        return builder.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)
73	    return builder.compile()
74	
75	
76	def _thread_config(
77	    run_id: str, recursion_limit: int, checkpoint_id: str | None = None
78	) -> dict[str, Any]:
79	    cfg: dict[str, Any] = {
80	        "configurable": {"thread_id": run_id},
81	        "recursion_limit": recursion_limit,
82	    }
83	    if checkpoint_id:
84	        cfg["configurable"]["checkpoint_id"] = checkpoint_id
85	    return cfg
86	
87	
88	def run(
89	    graph: Any,
90	    state: Any,
91	    *,
92	    run_id: str,
93	    recursion_limit: int = RECURSION_LIMIT,
94	    trace: bool = True,
95	) -> Any:
96	    """Invoke a compiled graph for ``run_id`` (the checkpointer thread id), within a trace
97	    context, bounded by ``recursion_limit``. The run parks at the first gate ``interrupt``;
98	    resume it with :func:`resume`."""
99	    if trace:
100	        bootstrap_tracing()
101	    config = _thread_config(run_id, recursion_limit)
102	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
103	        return graph.invoke(state, config=config)
104	
105	
106	def resume(
107	    graph: Any,
108	    *,
109	    run_id: str,
110	    gate_id: str,
111	    decision: str,
112	    rationale: str,
113	    recursion_limit: int = RECURSION_LIMIT,
114	    checkpoint_id: str | None = None,
115	    trace: bool = True,
116	) -> Any:
117	    """Resume a run parked at a gate with the operator's decision.
118	
119	    ``gate_id`` is the gate the operator intended to decide (from the governance channel) and
120	    is REQUIRED — the gate node's correlation guard rejects it if it does not match the parked
121	    gate, so a stale/misrouted decision cannot resume the wrong gate. Must use the SAME
122	    checkpointer the run started with (``thread_id == run_id``).
123	    """
124	    _require_langgraph()
125	    from langgraph.types import Command
126	
127	    if trace:
128	        bootstrap_tracing()
129	    config = _thread_config(run_id, recursion_limit, checkpoint_id)
130	    resume_value = {"gate_id": gate_id, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
131	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
132	        return graph.invoke(Command(resume=resume_value), config=config)
133

1	"""Adapter registration.
2	
3	A clone registers its :class:`~assay_engine.contracts.study.StudyDefinition` factory under a
4	name; the engine looks it up to run. This is the only place adapters and the engine meet by
5	name, and even here the engine holds only a factory callable — it imports no adapter module
6	itself (ARCHITECTURE.md §3). The instance repository imports the engine, builds its study,
7	and registers it.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import threading
13	from typing import Callable, Dict
14	
15	from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition
16	
17	StudyFactory = Callable[[], StudyDefinition]
18	
19	_REGISTRY: Dict[str, StudyFactory] = {}
20	# Registration is normally a one-shot import-time op, but the check-then-act duplicate guard
21	# is a TOCTOU; a lock makes it atomic if studies are ever registered concurrently (issue #13).
22	_LOCK = threading.Lock()
23	
24	
25	def register_study(name: str, factory: StudyFactory) -> None:
26	    """Register a study factory under ``name``. Raises on duplicate names."""
27	    with _LOCK:
28	        if name in _REGISTRY:
29	            raise ValueError(f"a study named {name!r} is already registered")
30	        _REGISTRY[name] = factory
31	
32	
33	def get_study(name: str) -> StudyDefinition:
34	    """Instantiate the registered study ``name``."""
35	    try:
36	        factory = _REGISTRY[name]
37	    except KeyError:
38	        raise KeyError(f"no study registered under {name!r}") from None
39	    return factory()
40	
41	
42	def registered_studies() -> tuple[str, ...]:
43	    return tuple(sorted(_REGISTRY))
44	
45	
46	def clear_registry() -> None:
47	    """Test helper: drop all registrations."""
48	    with _LOCK:
49	        _REGISTRY.clear()
50

1	"""Type-faithful canonical hashing — the engine's single content-digest primitive.
2	
3	Two unrelated places need a *stable, type-faithful* content hash: the baseline determinism
4	harness (so the same inputs reproduce the same baseline) and pre-registration (so a lock
5	binds the exact hypothesis content). Both must distinguish values that merely share a
6	``str()`` — ``int 1`` from ``str "1"``, ``date(2020,1,1)`` from ``"2020-01-01"`` — or the
7	hash is a liability rather than a guarantee.
8	
9	This module is the one implementation, hoisted here so it sits *below* both
10	``methodology`` and ``baseline`` in the import graph (neither may depend on the other). It is
11	dependency-free and pure. Anything it cannot canonicalize reproducibly is refused **loudly**:
12	a default (address-based) ``repr`` would make the digest differ every process and silently
13	break the guarantee it exists to provide.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import datetime as _dt
19	import decimal
20	import hashlib
21	import json
22	import uuid
23	from typing import Any, Mapping
24	
25	
26	def keytag(key: Any) -> str:
27	    """A type-faithful, sortable string for a mapping key, so distinct keys that happen to
28	    share a ``str()`` (e.g. int ``1`` vs str ``"1"``) do NOT collide."""
29	    return f"{type(key).__name__}::{key!r}"
30	
31	
32	def canonical_plain(value: Any) -> Any:
33	    """Canonicalize ``value`` into a fully JSON-native, type-faithful structure for hashing.
34	
35	    Mappings become a sorted list of ``[typed-key, value]`` pairs; bytes, sets, and any
36	    non-JSON-native leaf are tagged with their type rather than stringified. Nothing is coerced
37	    with ``str()``, so the content hash distinguishes values that share a ``str()``.
38	    """
39	    if value is None or isinstance(value, (str, bool, int, float)):
40	        return value  # JSON-native scalars; json encodes 1, "1", true, 1.0 distinctly
41	    if isinstance(value, bytes):
42	        return {"__bytes__": value.hex()}
43	    if isinstance(value, Mapping):
44	        pairs = sorted(
45	            ([keytag(k), canonical_plain(v)] for k, v in value.items()), key=lambda p: p[0]
46	        )
47	        return {"__map__": pairs}
48	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
49	        return [canonical_plain(v) for v in value]
50	    if isinstance(value, (set, frozenset)):
51	        return {"__set__": sorted((canonical_plain(v) for v in value), key=repr)}
52	    # A small allowlist of leaf types with a value-faithful canonical form.
53	    if isinstance(value, (_dt.date, _dt.datetime, _dt.time)):
54	        return {"__temporal__": value.isoformat()}
55	    if isinstance(value, decimal.Decimal):
56	        return {"__decimal__": str(value)}
57	    if isinstance(value, uuid.UUID):
58	        return {"__uuid__": str(value)}
59	    # Anything else is refused LOUDLY rather than repr-tagged: a default (address-based) repr
60	    # would make the digest differ every process, silently breaking the guarantee.
61	    raise TypeError(
62	        f"cannot canonicalize a leaf of type "
63	        f"{type(value).__module__}.{type(value).__qualname__!r} reproducibly — convert it to a "
64	        "JSON-native value, bytes, date/datetime/Decimal/UUID before hashing"
65	    )
66	
67	
68	def canonical_json(value: Any) -> str:
69	    """The canonical JSON text of ``value`` — stable across processes and key orderings."""
70	    return json.dumps(canonical_plain(value), sort_keys=True, allow_nan=False)
71	
72	
73	def hash_bytes(data: bytes) -> str:
74	    return hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()
75	
76	
77	def hash_text(text: str) -> str:
78	    return hash_bytes(text.encode("utf-8"))
79	
80	
81	def hash_value(value: Any) -> str:
82	    """Stable, type-faithful content hash of an arbitrary value.
83	
84	    ``canonical_plain`` canonicalizes everything to JSON-native form first, so ``json.dumps``
85	    needs no ``default=`` coercion — anything it still cannot encode is a real bug to surface,
86	    not to silently stringify.
87	    """
88	    return hash_text(canonical_json(value))
89

1	"""Deep-immutable, hashable mapping for the engine's frozen value records.
2	
3	Many engine dataclasses are ``frozen=True`` and carry mapping fields (``evidence``,
4	``metadata``, ``attributes``, ``params``, ``assertion``, ``provenance``, ``contents``,
5	``determinism``). With a plain ``dict`` default two problems arise (audit pass 1, issue #7):
6	
7	1. ``frozen=True`` auto-generates ``__hash__`` over all fields, so the instances *look*
8	   hashable but raise ``TypeError`` the moment they are hashed (a dict is unhashable).
9	2. ``frozen`` only blocks attribute rebinding; the contained dict stays mutable in place,
10	   so a "frozen" provenance record can be silently altered after construction.
11	
12	:class:`FrozenDict` fixes both: it is an immutable :class:`~collections.abc.Mapping` and is
13	hashable. :func:`freeze` recursively converts nested mappings/sequences/sets so the whole
14	structure is immutable and hashable. Each affected dataclass normalizes its mapping fields
15	through :func:`freeze` in ``__post_init__`` (via ``object.__setattr__`` to satisfy frozen).
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	from collections.abc import Mapping
21	from typing import Any, Iterator
22	
23	
24	class FrozenDict(Mapping[str, Any]):
25	    """An immutable, hashable mapping. Item assignment is impossible; hashing is stable."""
26	
27	    __slots__ = ("_data", "_hash")
28	
29	    def __init__(self, data: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> None:
30	        # Freeze values so a directly-constructed FrozenDict satisfies its own
31	        # immutable+hashable contract even from nested-mutable input (audit pass 3, issue #27).
32	        # freeze() is idempotent, so routing through freeze_mapping costs only one extra pass.
33	        self._data: dict[str, Any] = (
34	            {k: freeze(v) for k, v in data.items()} if data is not None else {}
35	        )
36	        self._hash: int | None = None
37	
38	    def __getitem__(self, key: str) -> Any:
39	        return self._data[key]
40	
41	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[str]:
42	        return iter(self._data)
43	
44	    def __len__(self) -> int:
45	        return len(self._data)
46	
47	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
48	        return f"FrozenDict({self._data!r})"
49	
50	    def __hash__(self) -> int:
51	        if self._hash is None:
52	            self._hash = hash(frozenset(self._data.items()))
53	        return self._hash
54	
55	    def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:
56	        if isinstance(other, Mapping):
57	            return dict(self._data) == dict(other)
58	        return NotImplemented
59	
60	
61	def freeze(value: Any) -> Any:
62	    """Recursively convert ``value`` into an immutable, hashable form.
63	
64	    Mappings → :class:`FrozenDict`, lists/tuples → tuples, sets → frozensets. Known mutable
65	    buffers are converted to hashable equivalents (``bytearray`` → ``bytes``;
66	    ``array.array`` / numpy-like arrays exposing ``tobytes`` → a ``(kind, shape, bytes)``
67	    tuple). Any remaining leaf that is not hashable raises ``TypeError`` *here, at
68	    construction time* — rather than silently passing through and resurfacing the error the
69	    moment the record is hashed (audit pass 2, issue #19; the incomplete half of #7).
70	    """
71	    if isinstance(value, FrozenDict):
72	        return value
73	    if isinstance(value, Mapping):
74	        return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})
75	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
76	        return tuple(freeze(v) for v in value)
77	    if isinstance(value, (set, frozenset)):
78	        return frozenset(freeze(v) for v in value)
79	    if isinstance(value, bytearray):
80	        return bytes(value)
81	    # Duck-typed buffer/array support (e.g. array.array, numpy.ndarray) without importing
82	    # numpy: an array-like exposing tobytes() is canonicalized to a hashable descriptor tuple.
83	    # Require an array-ish marker (shape/typecode/dtype) so an unrelated object that merely
84	    # happens to have a tobytes() is left to the hashability check below (fix-review nit, #19).
85	    tobytes = getattr(value, "tobytes", None)
86	    array_like = hasattr(value, "shape") or hasattr(value, "typecode") or hasattr(value, "dtype")
87	    if callable(tobytes) and array_like and not isinstance(value, (bytes, str)):
88	        shape = getattr(value, "shape", None)
89	        kind = getattr(getattr(value, "dtype", None), "str", None) or getattr(
90	            value, "typecode", type(value).__name__
91	        )
92	        # Route the descriptor through freeze() so a malformed tobytes() returning a
93	        # non-hashable value is still caught loud at construction (audit pass 3, issue #26).
94	        return freeze((str(kind), tuple(shape) if shape is not None else None, tobytes()))
95	    try:
96	        hash(value)
97	    except TypeError as exc:
98	        raise TypeError(
99	            f"freeze: leaf of type {type(value).__name__!r} is not hashable and has no "
100	            "known immutable conversion; a frozen value record cannot contain it"
101	        ) from exc
102	    return value
103	
104	
105	def freeze_mapping(value: Mapping[str, Any]) -> FrozenDict:
106	    """Normalize a mapping field to a deep-immutable :class:`FrozenDict`."""
107	    return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})
108
